text.title: Agentic RAG Self-Correction Core Rules
text.summary: 2 testable rules + 2 terminal conclusions every self-correction loop must enforce.

rule.statement: Faithfulness verifier MUST use a different model than the generator (or different temperature with explicit prompt distancing). When verifier reports >2 ungrounded claims, block surfacing the answer.
rule.rationale: Same model confirms its own outputs; using a different model catches hallucinations the generator missed.

rule.statement: Self-correction capped at `max_corrections` (default 2). On cap reached without verified answer, log full iteration trace and escalate to human review.
rule.rationale: Uncapped loops drift and burn budget; humans must see traces.

rule.statement: Hallucination >3% AND distinct verifier available AND latency budget supports 1–3s overhead → wire self-correct.
Hallucination <1% OR only one model available OR latency <2s → skip; verifier overhead exceeds gain.
